channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Home entertainment holiday gift ideas at a discount
item.description: The industry buzz is we will see repeats between now and Christmas so if you miss the deal first time around, keep checking.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Everything leaving Netflix in December 2024
item.description: As the year draws to a close, Netflix waves goodbye to a few more titles, but thankfully there's not much leaving the streamer in December 2024.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Music Revenues Now Higher Than Global Box Office Receipts: A Report Reveals
item.description: Music industry revenues have surpassed global box office earnings, with streaming and vinyl sales driving growth, despite artists not profiting from these changes.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cryptocurrency entrepreneur who bought banana art for $6.2 million eats the fruit in Hong Kong
item.description: A cryptocurrency entrepreneur who had bought a piece of conceptual art consisting of a simple banana, duct-taped to a wall, for $6.2 million last week ate the fruit in Hong Kong on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: My spur-of-the-moment Chromebook surprise
item.description: If you’ve read this column for long, you probably know that when it comes to tech purchases — and tech decisions in general, really — I’m typically not one to be hasty. It’s practically in my blood at this point.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Joshua trees are in peril. California has a plan to save them
item.description: The California Department of Fish and Wildlife has released a new plan to protect the state’s iconic Joshua trees, which are imperiled by wildfires, human development and climate change.
item.dc:creator: Dorany Pineda, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US police departments put EV patrol cars to the test
item.description: Some local police departments are considering moving to electric vehicles to bolster their fleet of patrol vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Uber branches out into AI data labeling
item.description: Uber no longer offers just rides and deliveries: It’s created a new division hiring out gig workers to help enterprises with some of their AI model development work.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: One Tech Tip: Home for the holidays? Show relatives you care with some tech support
item.description: As the holiday season kicks off, the journey home could also mean that those who are more tech-savvy will be called on to provide a backlog of tech support to parents, grandparents and other family.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Chen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FTC opens antitrust investigation into Microsoft’s cloud, AI, and cybersecurity practices
item.description: The US Federal Trade Commission (FTC) has launched an antitrust investigation into Microsoft, scrutinizing its cloud computing, software licensing, cybersecurity, and AI businesses. The inquiry follows over a year of informal interviews with competitors and business partners, Bloomberg reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use ref structs in C# 13
item.description: In the C# programming language, structs or structure types are value types that allow faster access because they are usually stored in the stack.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kai Trump shows Elon Musk awkwardly tutoring Trump in rocket science
item.description: While Trump's aspiring influencer granddaughter happily shared video of her joining him at Musk's latest SpaceX launch, she also revealed moments of the president-elect looking 'out of depth' and 'intimidated.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Amazon's $4B bet on an AI startup
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok rolling out age restrictions on beauty filters amid mental health concerns
item.description: TikTok announced Tuesday it will restrict the use of some appearance effects for users under 18 after teens and parents in a new study expressed concerns about the impact of beauty filters.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: For Black Friday deals, these 3 sites are all you need
item.description: I’m in my mid-forties. Things are going gray in places and my hips hurt for no reason. And back in my day, Black Friday was one day: the Friday after Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Python to C: What’s new in Cython 3.1
item.description: Cython is the most convenient way to bridge C and Python languages and tools in Python programs. Developers use it to write C extensions or wrap C libraries for use in Python. Now, a major new release is on the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Kotlin to lose scripting features
item.description: JetBrains plans to drop some scripting-related technologies from the Kotlin language, including REPL functionality, an obsolete API for Java scripting, the KotlinScriptMojo Maven plugin, and the kotlin-scripting-ide-services library for implementing code completion functionality.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Starbucks and other retailers hit with ongoing ransomware attack on software provider
item.description: Major supply-chain software provider Blue Yonder is working to restore its systems after a ransomware attack hit the Panasonic-owned firm last week. Blue Yonder, which counts Starbucks, major U.K.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How to (kindly) ask people not to post your kids' photos on social media
item.description: With kids facing a seemingly increasing amount of threats from creeps on the internet, here's how you can respectfully ask people not to post photos of your children on social media.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explicit ‘deepfake' images of nearly 50 teens rocks Pennsylvania school
item.description: Students have used AI technology to create fake explicit images of classmates in Washington, California and New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Anthropic introduces the Model Context Protocol
item.description: Anthropic today released a new open source protocol to let all AI systems, not just its own, connect with data sources via a standard interface.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Interpol arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages, the global police organization said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience
item.description: Sheena Burrell, who was NARA's chief information officer until Nov. 21, said the agency is upgrading systems to deliver a modernized user experience. The post NARA to launch AI tool to improve customer experience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Is Outlook down? Thousands of Microsoft 365 users report outage issues
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Thousands of Microsoft 365 customers worldwide reported having issues with services like Outlook and Teams on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
